rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. PETER KAUTZMANN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Agreement Pursuant To Ccp 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 7, 2021, line 1. DEFENDANT PETER KAUTZMANN's Motion To Enforce Agreement Pursuant To Ccp 664.6. OFF CALENDAR. The Court has no jurisdiction to hear this motion. Action has been dismissed to order retaining jurisdiction was sought and obtained prior to the dismissal. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ANDINA PROPERTY LLC VS. LAURA FOX ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 7, 2021, line 2. DEFENDANT EDWARD-LEOPOLD: NOVAK's HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION. Hearing Required. Claimant to bring all evidence supporting his claim that he was in possession as of June 4, 2019 per a rental agreement with the former owner of the property including but not limited to copies of the rental agreement, rent payment receipts, cancelled checks, utility bills, school enrollment, paychecks or government assistance checks reflecting the address at the premises prior to June 4, 2019, etc. All evidence to be provided to the courtroom clerks no later than 9:00 a.m. on September 7, 2021 and filed in room 103 by 9:30 a.m. on September 7, 2021. rulings.Case Title: HENRY YIP VS. AYANA BASTAIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Defendants Motion To Vacate Judgment And Recall And Quash The Writ Of Execution
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 7, 2021, line 3. DEFENDANT GEORGENA BELL's Motion To Vacate Judgment And Recall And Quash The Writ Of Execution. DENIED. The Court takes into consideration all filings by the moving party in this action. Moving party failed to show that the default was entered due to lack of actual notice of the action or excusable neglect. This action commenced on February 20, 2020. Moving party claims that she "did not fully understand what [she] needed to do at that time to protect [her] rights." Moving party first appeared in this action on August 24, 2021, 18 months have passed since the moving party did not fully understand how to protect her rights. This neglect is inexcusable. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BAYVIEW PRESERVATION, L.P. VS. CHARLES LOPEZ-MONTES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 7, 2021, line 4. DEFENDANT CHARLES LOPEZ-MONTES' DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. Notice is fatally defective and providing inconsistent compliance/termination date: (1) January 29, 2020 expressly stated; and (3) method of calculation of the date of termination/compliance, falling on January 30, 2020. Opposition does not address this issue. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: FERNANDO LOPEZ VS. ERNEST LOPEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 7, 2021, line 6. DEFENDANT ERNEST LOPEZ's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Demurrer tests the sufficiency of the allegations in the complaint. Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that an alleged Power-of-Attorney cannot verify the complaint for the Plaintiff. Whether this allegation is true and whether Plaintiff possesses evidence on this point is an issue for another proceeding. Notice is expressly based on CCP 1161(4) and defendant does not cite any authority for the proposition that weekends and holidays are excluded for the purposes of 3-day calculation under CCP 1161(4). Demurrer cannot be asserted against a part of a cause of action and Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that it may be asserted as to the part of the Notice. Assuming, that the Notice improperly demanded payment of utility bills without COVID-19 applicable requirements, the sufficiency of the rest of the notice is not addressed in the demurrer. The premises are described in the incorporated lease and diagram. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)
